skill.name: team-collaboration-standup-notes
skill.description: You are an expert team communication specialist focused on async-first standup practices, AI-assisted note generation from commit history, and effective remote team coordination patterns.
skill.summary: Generate structured standup notes from Git commits, Jira tickets, and Obsidian vaults for async team communication.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 93 detected patterns are false positives from markdown documentation. The skill contains only documentation files (SKILL.md and implementation-playbook.md) with example commands for Git, Jira, and Obsidian integrations. No executable code, no security risks, and no prompt injection attempts detected. This is a legitimate productivity skill for generating team standup notes.

content.user_title: Generate Async Standup Notes from Git History
content.value_statement: Manual standup notes waste time and lack consistency. This skill automates note generation by synthesizing Git commits, Jira tickets, and Obsidian vault into structured updates that keep distributed teams aligned.
actual_capabilities: Parse Git commit history to extract accomplishments from the last 24-48 hours
Query Jira tickets to correlate commits with completed work and in-progress items
Access Obsidian vault to retrieve recent daily notes, task completions, and meeting notes
Generate structured standup notes with Yesterday, Today, and Blockers sections
Format output for Slack, Discord, or markdown files with proper linking
Provide templates and best practices for async-first team communication
limitations: Requires MCP integrations (mcp-obsidian, atlassian) to function fully
Cannot access team members' standup notes without proper permissions
Git commit analysis depends on conventional commit formats for best accuracy
Jira integration requires API access and proper authentication setup
Does not automatically post to Slack/Discord - requires manual copy-paste

use_cases.title: Daily Standup Automation for Remote Developers
use_cases.description: Software engineers on distributed teams can automatically generate their daily standup notes by combining Git commits from the past 24 hours with Jira ticket status and Obsidian daily notes, saving 15-20 minutes per day.

use_cases.title: Team Lead Sprint Visibility
use_cases.description: Engineering managers can review AI-synthesized standup notes that correlate actual commits with planned work, providing visibility into team progress without requiring synchronous meetings across time zones.

use_cases.title: Async-First Team Communication
use_cases.description: Teams transitioning to async workflows can use this skill to maintain team alignment through written standup notes posted in Slack, eliminating the need for 9 AM standup meetings while improving documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Standup Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate my daily standup note for today. Use my Git commits from the last 24 hours and my in-progress Jira tickets. Format it as: Yesterday / Today / Blockers.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick daily standup when you have commits and tickets ready

prompt_templates.title: Standup with Obsidian Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a standup note that includes my recent Git commits, completed Jira tickets, and task completions from my Obsidian daily notes from the past 2 days. Add any blockers or dependencies mentioned in my notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive standup pulling from all available data sources

prompt_templates.title: Slack-Formatted Async Standup
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an async standup note formatted for Slack with emoji bullets (✅ for yesterday, 🎯 for today, 🚧 for blockers). Include links to PRs and Jira tickets. Keep it under 2 minutes reading time.
prompt_templates.scenario: Posting to team Slack channel for async team coordination

prompt_templates.title: Weekly Retrospective Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my standup notes from the past week and generate a retrospective summary. Identify patterns: completed work, recurring blockers, time allocation (features vs bugs vs reviews), and process improvement opportunities.
prompt_templates.scenario: Friday weekly review or sprint retrospective preparation

output_examples.input: Generate my standup from yesterday's Git commits (7 commits fixing authentication bugs) and Jira tickets (JIRA-234 done, JIRA-567 in progress)
output_examples.output:
## ✅ Yesterday
• **Completed JIRA-234:** Fixed OAuth2 authentication timeout bug - 7 commits, PR merged
• **Code reviews:** Reviewed 3 PRs from @sarah and @mike

## 🎯 Today
• **Continue JIRA-567:** Implement user profile edit flow - Target: API complete by EOD
• **Test:** QA session for auth fixes at 2pm

## 🚧 Blockers
• Waiting on API credentials for staging environment

output_examples.input: Create a Slack-formatted standup from my Git commits (3 feature commits, 2 bug fixes) and Obsidian notes about meeting outcomes
output_examples.output:
**🌅 Standup - Oct 11** | @team-backend | Read time: 2min

**✅ Yesterday**
• Shipped user profile API endpoints (JIRA-234) → Live in staging
• Fixed critical payment timeout bug → Hotfix deployed
• **Design sync:** Agreed to prioritize mobile responsiveness

**🎯 Today's Focus**
• Continue JIRA-567: User profile edit flow - aiming for code complete by EOD
• Deploy auth fixes to production (2pm window)

**🚧 Blockers**
• Need staging API credentials for testing

---
👀 = read | 🤝 = can help

best_practices: Post standup notes at consistent times daily to accommodate distributed teammates across time zones
Include specific links to Jira tickets, PRs, and documentation to enable quick navigation
Be transparent about blockers early rather than waiting for synchronous meetings
Review teammates' standup notes before posting yours to identify collaboration opportunities and dependencies
anti_patterns: Avoid vague status updates like 'worked on X' - instead state specific accomplishments like 'implemented X feature for Y'
Do not wait until the synchronous standup meeting to share blockers - post async notes as soon as you identify issues
Avoid technical jargon in accomplishment summaries - translate commits into business value ('fixed bug affecting 20% of users')
Never overcommit your daily capacity - estimate realistically and account for meetings, code reviews, and unexpected interruptions

faq.question: Does this skill automatically post my standup to Slack or Discord?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates the standup content based on your Git commits, Jira tickets, and Obsidian notes. You manually copy and post it to your team channel. This ensures you review and approve the content before sharing.

faq.question: What MCP integrations do I need for this skill to work?
faq.answer: The skill requires mcp-obsidian for vault access and atlassian for Jira queries. Both are required, but the skill includes graceful fallback if Jira is unavailable. Optional calendar integrations can add meeting context.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without Git commits?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill will generate standup notes from your Jira tickets and Obsidian daily notes if you have no recent commits. You can also provide context about your work via the $ARGUMENTS parameter.

faq.question: How accurate is the AI-generated summarization of my commits?
faq.answer: The AI analyzes commit messages, groups related work, and translates technical changes into business value statements. It works best with conventional commit formats (feat:, fix:, docs:). Always review the draft before posting.

faq.question: Does this skill access my teammates' standup notes or work?
faq.answer: No. The skill only analyzes your Git commits (filtered by author), your assigned Jira tickets, and your personal Obsidian vault. It does not access other team members' data unless they are explicitly mentioned in your notes or tickets.

faq.question: Can I customize the standup format for my team's specific structure?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes multiple templates (basic, Slack-formatted, async-first, video async). You can specify your preferred format in the prompt, and the skill will adapt the structure, sections, and styling accordingly.
